summaryPoints: Conversational multi-agent bioimage analysis for ImageJ and beyond.
Natural-language requests are translated into executable and verifiable workflows.
ImageJ/Fiji, Python scientific tools, and deep-learning models are orchestrated in one system.
summaryPointsEn: Conversational multi-agent bioimage analysis for ImageJ and beyond.
Natural-language requests are translated into executable and verifiable workflows.
ImageJ/Fiji, Python scientific tools, and deep-learning models are orchestrated in one system.

background.title: Background
items: Quantitative bioimage analysis is essential but remains difficult to scale with manual ImageJ workflows and macro scripting.
Researchers face fragmented documentation, parameter-heavy tools, and limited access to modern Python and deep-learning methods.
items_en: Quantitative bioimage analysis is essential but remains difficult to scale with manual ImageJ workflows and macro scripting.
Researchers face fragmented documentation, parameter-heavy tools, and limited access to modern Python and deep-learning methods.

we_present.title: We Present
items: CopilotJ is a conversational multi-agent system that plans, executes, validates, and documents ImageJ-centric workflows.
It supports reproducible workflow reuse, batch execution, human-in-the-loop interaction, and deep-learning extensions.
items_en: CopilotJ is a conversational multi-agent system that plans, executes, validates, and documents ImageJ-centric workflows.
It supports reproducible workflow reuse, batch execution, human-in-the-loop interaction, and deep-learning extensions.

challenges.title: Challenges
items: ImageJ analysis often depends on user expertise, fragmented plugin knowledge, and repeated trial-and-error.
Extending workflows with external Python or deep-learning tools usually increases engineering overhead for end users.
items_en: ImageJ analysis often depends on user expertise, fragmented plugin knowledge, and repeated trial-and-error.
Extending workflows with external Python or deep-learning tools usually increases engineering overhead for end users.
